songs.subject: night shift warehouse, rap/flow
songs.lyrics:
Clocking in at midnight, staring at the dim light
Gotta get my mind right, stepping on the line tight
Heavy lifting, time shifts, moving through the blind clips
Pallets on the iron lifts, crushing on my spine shifts
Cold steel, old wheels, grinding out what's real now
Thinking 'bout the bills now, working for the meals now
Stacking up the inventory, masking up a broken story
No one sees the faded glory, pacing on this concrete floor we
Keep on pushing, never resting, every single muscle testing
Stress is on my chest pressing, looking for a hidden blessing
Tearing through the cardboard, praying to the Lord more
Wondering what I stored for, fighting in this quiet war
But I see my daughter sleeping, keep the promises I'm keeping
Even when the shadows creeping, even when the pain is deeping
Got a family to carry, and the weight is getting heavy
But my hands are holding steady, yeah, I'm ready, yeah, I'm ready.

Night shift, light shift, trying just to make it through
Everything I break in two is everything I do for you
Forklifts screaming, but I'm looking at a dream ahead
Working till my hands are red, keeping everybody fed
Midnight to the sunrise, blinking out the dust and grit
Never gonna quit the script, I'm fighting for the life we get.

Three AM is freezing, everybody wheezing
Fluorescent lights are teasing, looking for a season easing
Cardboard and tape tearing, supervisors swearing
I don't care what coat I'm wearing, it's the burden that I'm bearing
Pack the freight, track the weight, never can be slacking late
Got to dictate the fate, open up a better gate
My son needs the shoes, man, I cannot afford to lose, man
Walking through the blues, man, making all the right moves, man
Blisters on my fingers, but the motivation lingers
Listen to the bells and ringers, time is just a thief that injures
But the overtime is climbing, matching with my heartbeat timing
Looking for the silver lining, while the forklift gears are whining
Breakroom is a ghost town, coffee got me host now
Focus on the folks now, who really need the most now
Sun is finally breaking through the dock doors in the back
Time to pull another pack, put the future on the track.

Night shift, light shift, trying just to make it through
Everything I break in two is everything I do for you
Forklifts screaming, but I'm looking at a dream ahead
Working till my hands are red, keeping everybody fed
Midnight to the sunrise, blinking out the dust and grit
Never gonna quit the script, I'm fighting for the life we get.
